x-enumeration-literals.name: Account Credit Delivery Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A delivery method where the transferred funds are credited directly to the beneficiarys bank account.

Comment:
Example:

A customer transfers €500 to another persons savings account, and the money is credited to that account.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Cash Pickup Delivery Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A delivery method where the beneficiary collects the transferred money in physical cash from a bank branch or authorized agent.

Comment:
Example:

A sender transfers €300 through a remittance service, and the recipient collects the money in cash at a branch.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Mobile Wallet Delivery Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A delivery method where the funds are transferred to the beneficiarys mobile wallet or digital wallet account.

Comment:
Example:

Sending €100 to a mobile wallet account through a mobile banking application.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Card Credit Delivery Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A delivery method where the transferred funds are credited directly to a debit or credit card account.

Comment:
Example:

A refund of €200 credited back to the customers debit card.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Cheque Delivery Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A delivery method where the funds are delivered through a cheque issued to the beneficiary.

Comment:
Example:

A bank issues a cheque of €1,000 to a beneficiary as payment.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Third-Party Delivery Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A delivery method where the funds are delivered through an authorized intermediary such as a payment agent or money transfer operator.

Comment:
Example:

A transfer sent through a money transfer company, and the recipient collects the funds from an authorized agent.
